disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Loss of consciousness occurred while the patient was asleep and lying flat in bed
trigger_question_examples: What was he doing when it happened?
Was he lying down or standing when he passed out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He was fast asleep in bed. There was no warning — he just went completely limp and unresponsive.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: During the episode there was loud gasping, agonal-type noisy breathing lasting about a minute
trigger_question_examples: What did the breathing sound like during the episode?
Was he breathing normally while he was out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He was making horrible loud gasping, snoring-type noises — not normal breathing at all — for about a minute, and then it settled as he came round.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A first-degree relative (brother) died suddenly and unexpectedly in his sleep at age 30
trigger_question_examples: Has anyone in your family died suddenly or unexpectedly, especially when young?
Any family history of heart problems or unexplained deaths?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — my brother died in his sleep at 30. They never found out why. I've never connected it to anything until now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Current febrile illness (feverish with a mild upper respiratory tract infection) at the time of the event
trigger_question_examples: Have you been unwell or had a fever recently?
Any cold or infection going on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had a bit of a head cold the last day or so and felt feverish and shivery tonight — that's why I took the paracetamol.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid spontaneous recovery with brief post-event confusion; no sustained jerking, no tongue-biting, no incontinence witnessed
trigger_question_examples: Was there any jerking or stiffening of his body?
Did he bite his tongue or wet himself, and how long until he was back to normal?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No real jerking that I saw, no bitten tongue, no wetting. He was groggy and confused for about ten minutes and then he was completely himself again.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No preceding chest pain, palpitations, prodrome, or prior syncopal episodes
trigger_question_examples: Did you feel any chest pain, a racing heart, or warning before it happened?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain and no racing heart that I noticed, but honestly I was asleep. I've never fainted before in my life.
